title: Contested territory: a theory of land and democracy beyond sovereign bounds [Book Review]
author: Utrata

opening_move.description: The review opens by framing Jurkevics's intervention against cosmopolitan political theory: she shares cosmopolitanism's rejection of sovereignty but recovers the concept of territory as the site of a richer, localised democratic politics.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Cosmopolitans have abandoned territory along with sovereignty, losing the most promising sites for thick democratic politics; territory can be salvaged for emancipatory purposes

theoretical_positioning.description: The review presents Jurkevics's argument appreciatively, framing her recovery of territory as a corrective to cosmopolitan and anarchical theory without adding independent critique.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: close reading of Jurkevics and canonical political theory texts
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review genre; primarily expository and appreciative.

confidence_notes: Book review genre; primarily expository. The reviewer focuses on accurately presenting Jurkevics's argument rather than independently engaging it.
